rag_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
generic_rules: Validate all generated AGREE clauses against the current component's visible identifiers
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not invent declarations for unresolved identifiers; use existing visible identifiers
Preserve the original AADL architecture when repairing AGREE clauses
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assign output = expression;` for implementation-level bindings
Use `guarantee "label": expression;` for output dependency contracts in type scope
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees that refer to subcomponent ports in component type scope
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
Do not invent declarations for unresolved identifiers
